title: Satellite Spots a Spawn
summary: The activity of herring around Vancouver Island in British Columbia brightened coastal waters enough to be detectable from space.

title: U.S. GSSAP satellites execute GEO handoff to monitor China’s Shijian-29 spacecraft
summary:
Commercial space tracking data shows U.S. satellites coordinating maneuvers to maintain proximity and continuous observation of a pair of Chinese spacecraft in geostationary orbit.

title: What’s Up: April 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: Mercury shines at its brightest for the year, the Lyrid meteor shower peaks, and a bright new comet makes an appearance in April’s night sky.

title: Government use of commercial procurement models has limitations in space
summary:
While government agencies are increasingly turning to commercial contracting mechanisms for space capabilities, that does not necessarily translate to additional business opportunities for the companies offering them.

title: NASA to Unveil Complete Roman Telescope, Host Media Briefing
summary: Media are invited Tuesday, April 21, to NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, for a look at the agency’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, which recently completed construction and is wrapping up prelaunch testing. This will be one of the last opportunities to view the fully integrated flagship telescope before it ships to […]

title: NASA Releases Artemis II Moon Mission Launch Countdown
summary: Before NASA sends its astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen on their Artemis II mission around the Moon, the launch team at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida and teams across the country will begin counting down about two days before liftoff.  A launch countdown contains […]

title: Station Nation: Aaron Rose, Cold Stowage Mission Manager
summary: As a member of the Crew and Thermal Systems Division, Aaron Rose supports critical cargo resupply missions to the International Space Station. In this role, he works with payload developers to safely transport temperature-controlled science experiments to and from station with portable coolers, freezers, and refrigerators.  For the full flight cycle, Rose and his team members ensure all cold stowage hardware, operations, and personnel are coordinated to […]

title: Turning growth into profits remains a challenge as space demand grows
summary:
